SUSTAINED WITH 20 DAYS LEAVE TO AMEND AS TO CAUSE OF ACTION 6 TO PLEAD CONTRACT AND MALPRACTICE CLAIMS SEPARATELY AND TO PLEAD TERMS OF THE ALLEGED CONTRACTS AND HOW THEY WERE BREACHED. AMENDED DEMURRER TO 2ND AMENDED COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Aug-08-2007 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: Appearances:
Kara E. Farmer of Hinshaw & Culbertson, 362-6000, appearing for defendant and moving party
John Mounier of the Mounier Law Firm, 332-6569, appearing for plaintiff and responding party

Argued and the Court modified its tentative ruling as follows: DEFENDANT NANCY FIELD'S AMENDED DEMURRER TO 2ND AMENDED COMPLAINT is sustained without leave to amend as to causes of action 1, 2, and 3. Sustained with 20 days leave to amend as to cause of action 4 to plead facts showing reliance. Overruled as to cause of action 5. Sustained with 20 days leave to amend as to cause of action 6 to plead contract and malpractice claims separately and to plead terms of the alleged contracts and how they were breached. Otherwise overruled. Prevailing party to prepare a form of order. (301/PJB/PM) Judge: Peter J. Busch, Reporter: Carol Karen, CSR 8189

Mtn And Notice Of Mtn Toc Ompel Pl'S Responses To Df'S Special Interrogatories (Set One) And Requests For Production Of Doucments (Set One) And Request For Monetary Sanctions
Off Calendar Aug-06-2007 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
Appearance:
Kara E. Farmer, Esq., appeared for Defendant & moving party: Nancy Field
No other appearance
Ruling:
Hearing held and tentative ruling adopted: Defendant Nancy Field's Motion To Compel Plaintiff's Responses To Defendant's Special Interrogatories (Set One) And Requests For Production Of Documents (Set One) is granted. Plaintiff is ordered to respond within 15 days of notice of this order. The court finds that Plaintiff's responses were a misuse of the discovery process. Sanctions are imposed against Plaintiff in the amount of $1,440, to be paid within 30 days of notice of this order. Order signed in open court. COMM: EVERETT A. HEWLETT
